prompt:
A compact 1200 W heat gun rests on a soldering-safe work surface, its nozzle pointing away from nearby components and its built-in stand deployed to keep the hot end elevated. A simple control switch on the handle shows two heat settings, but the markings are too small to read clearly, suggesting different temperature levels without giving exact numbers. A length of heat-shrink tubing on a wire nearby is partially shrunken, clearly demonstrating the tool’s use for shrinking tubing rather than heavy industrial work.

The environment is a near-future solarpunk electronics and repair station with soft, even lighting, a silicone mat under the work area, and organized tools and parts on shelves in the blurred background. The 512×512 image is photorealistic with gentle stylization, no logos or readable labels on the heat gun, and a composition that centers the gun, its stand, and the shrunken tubing while the rest of the workspace remains softly out of focus to emphasize safe, controlled use.
